decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. Following the strategy shift suggestion to use coordinated multi-variable changes, I will simultaneously increase the heat spreader's thermal conductivity and its physical dimensions (width and height). This combines material enhancement with geometric expansion to maximize lateral heat spreading and reduce thermal resistance.

changes.reason: Increase conductivity by factor of 1.25 (within 2.0x limit) to improve lateral heat distribution across the spreader.
Widen spreader to maximum allowed bound (1.0m) to capture more heat from the chip and spread it towards the cooled boundaries.
Increase spreader thickness by 50% to provide a larger cross-sectional area for heat conduction, reducing vertical thermal resistance.

expected_effects: Significant reduction in chip peak temperature due to combined material and geometric improvements.
More uniform temperature distribution across the top layer.
risk_notes: Aggressive geometry changes may push the spreader close to domain boundaries, limiting future expansion options.
Diminishing returns on conductivity increases if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate or boundary conditions.
Need to verify no geometric overlap occurs with other components after resizing (though current layout suggests clearance exists).
